items.title: Full pipeline — occlusion recovery to criticality
items.description: The complete pipeline run end to end: the occlusion-recovered road mask, its skeleton, and the resulting criticality analysis overlaid on the source tile. The red segment is the single point of failure for this local network.

items.title: Criticality analysis on forest terrain
items.description: The same criticality pipeline run on a vegetation-heavy tile. Because detection is sparse here, the resulting graph fragments into 7 disconnected components — a direct, honest illustration of why occlusion-robust extraction matters for graph analysis.

items.title: Occlusion recovery with clean reference
items.description: Fusing a clean reference pass into an occluded tile to restore the missing road mask.

items.title: Final fused road network
items.description: The final continuous road network after morphological closing reconnects the broken segments.

items.title: Raw confidence heatmap
items.description: The un-thresholded probabilities from the U-Net model on a challenging tile.

items.title: Confidence threshold sweep
items.description: Sweeping the confidence threshold down from 0.5 to 0.08 reveals that faint unpaved roads are not recovered, indicating a data limitation rather than a tuning issue.

items.title: Baseline forest extraction
items.description: A baseline extraction on heavy canopy, showing the typical gaps that corrupt downstream graph analysis.
